Move model selector below Cloud radio, fix layout visibility

- Model selector (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) now appears directly below the
  Cloud radio button and is hidden when API mode is active

  reflow and repainting on Haiku

  values across separate parent groups

  persistence behaviour

commit.message:
fix: 4 bugs introduced by other agent session

- OrganizationContext: add enabled:isAuthenticated to prevent 401 storm
  on login page (was firing api.organizations.getMe unconditionally)
- sites.py POST: create OrgSite junction row so list_sites (which queries
  OrgSite) returns newly created sites for the org
- AIAgents.tsx: fix dead newMessages variable + stale closure; move
  localStorage history update inside setMessages updater so it uses
  current state, not stale closure
- test_e2e_saas_flow.py: update plan assertion from 'starter' to match
  register endpoint which now sets plan='free', plan_status='active'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: address security, bug, and dead code issues from code review

- Use hmac.compare_digest for timing-safe admin key comparison
- Add hash length validation (max 64 chars) on webhook data
- Make dequeue atomic with UPDATE+subquery to prevent TOCTOU race
- Strip UTF-8 BOM in looks_like_html content sniffing
- Use script.get_text() instead of .string to handle child elements
- Return None for ambiguous content types (octet-stream, text/plain)
- Handle confirmed_malicious overrides in backfill sweeps
- Add reset_failed() for retrying failed queue items
- Remove unused health_check from GatewayClient

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add support for pre-configured OAuth client credentials (#36)

* feat: add --client-id and --client-secret options to login command

For servers that don't support dynamic client registration, users can
now provide pre-configured OAuth client credentials via CLI flags:

  mcpc login mcp.example.com --client-id XXX --client-secret YYY

When provided, the credentials are stored in the keychain and used
directly for the OAuth flow, bypassing dynamic client registration.
The client metadata auth method is set to client_secret_post when a
secret is provided (confidential client), otherwise remains none
(public client).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SQSYrXbVmLATkqq59zkSY8

* style: fix prettier formatting

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SQSYrXbVmLATkqq59zkSY8

commit.message:
fix(web): upgrade eslint-plugin-svelte 2→3 to fix peer dep conflict with eslint 10

eslint-plugin-svelte v2 only supports eslint ≤9, causing npm ci to fail
on Railway. v3 supports eslint 10. Disabled new v3 rules (require-each-key,
no-navigation-without-resolve, prefer-svelte-reactivity) to keep existing
behavior.

commit.message:
feat: auto-scroll carousels on mobile/tablet for all horizontal sections

NEW src/components/motion/AutoScrollCarousel.tsx
  - 'use client' wrapper component; transparent pass-through on desktop
  - requestAnimationFrame loop at 0.5 px/frame (≈30 px/s smooth crawl)
  - IntersectionObserver: pauses rAF when section is off-screen (saves CPU)
  - Pauses on touchstart / touchmove / touchend / mousedown / wheel
  - Resumes after 2500 ms of inactivity from where the user left off
  - Sub-pixel accumulator ensures no stuttering at fractional speeds
  - Loops seamlessly: scrollLeft resets to 0 when end is reached

globals.css — carousel breakpoint extended from 768px → 1023px
  - Horizontal scroll now active on both mobile AND tablet (< lg)
  - Swipe-hint label visible at ≤ 1023px
  - Tablet card widths (768–1023px): 60% container width
  - Mobile card widths (< 768px): 80% container width
  - Productions portrait cards: 45% tablet / 68% mobile

Applied AutoScrollCarousel to 6 sections (desktop grid unchanged):
  Sets, Gallery, ProductionTypes, BehindScenes, Testimonials, Productions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: block avatar save when image persistence fails

Previously handleSaveAvatar() silently saved with a temporary URL when
both persistence attempts failed, leading to broken avatar images once
the URL expired. Now it shows a destructive toast and blocks the save
so the user can retry or upload directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

R-17: Use circle checkboxes for checklist items, match todo item styling

Checklist items now use circle checkboxes (same as the main todo toggle)
instead of squares, so they feel like first-class todo items rather than
subordinate sub-tasks. Bumped font sizes to body weight for equal visual
prominence.

commit.message:
Research: BSD + NS — comprehensive additions (XXVIII-XXXVII, XXIV-XXIX) (#3413)

* research: BSD Cassels-Tate pairing, Iwasawa theory, p-adic BSD, Tunnell's theorem (Parts XXVIII-XXXIII)

- Cassels-Tate alternating pairing on Ш: proved antisymmetry, |Ш| is perfect square
- Iwasawa theory: μ/λ invariants, Main Conjecture (Kato + Skinner-Urban)
- p-adic BSD conjecture: Mazur-Swinnerton-Dyer p-adic L-function, ℒ-invariant
- Tunnell's theorem: congruent number criterion with verified examples (n=1,2,3,5,6)
- Rank records (Elkies ≥ 28), Mestre construction, distribution summary
- Modular symbols: computational BSD via [0]⁺, Cremona database verification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: NS Prodi-Serrin endpoint (ESS 2003), Besov spaces, multifractal structure (Parts XXIV-XXVI)

- ESS theorem: L^∞_t L^3_x regularity via backward uniqueness
- Serrin exponent parameterization: p = 2q/(q-3), criticality proved
- Besov spaces: critical smoothness -1+3/p, chain H^{1/2} > L^3 > BMO^{-1} proved
- Onsager-Besov threshold B^{1/3}_{3,∞} formalized
- She-Levêque: ζ_3 = 1, ζ_6 = 16/9 proved, intermittency δ = 2/9 proved
- K41 singularity spectrum: D(1/3) = 3 proved
- CKN partial regularity: singular set dim ≤ 1, gap = 1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update BSD and NS knowledge bases with new insights

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: BSD Bloch-Kato generalization, Euler systems, explicit computations (Parts XXXV-XXXVII)

- Bloch-Kato conjecture: motives framework, BSD as special case PROVED
- Euler systems: Kolyvagin, Kato, Rubin's machine, rank barrier at 2
- Explicit computations: AGM periods, L-value ratios, full BSD verification
  template with y²=x³-x and 37a as worked examples

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update BSD knowledge with Bloch-Kato, Euler systems, explicit computations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: NS convex integration, non-uniqueness (Buckmaster-Vicol), Type I/II singularities (Parts XXVIII-XXIX)

- Convex integration: De Lellis-Székelyhidi framework, Isett (Onsager resolved)
- Buckmaster-Vicol 2019: Leray-Hopf weak solutions are NOT unique
- Selection criteria: 5 candidates (all OPEN for uniqueness)
- Temporal intermittency: overcoming viscosity in CI scheme
- Type I singularities EXCLUDED via ESŠ + Nečas-Růžička-Šverák
- Type II: the remaining open frontier for Millennium Prize
- Quantitative regularity: Tao's approach
- Millennium Prize precisely restated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update NS knowledge with convex integration, singularity classification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: YM Gribov copies, chiral anomaly, dual superconductor confinement (Parts XLIII-XLV)

- Gribov copies: Singer's theorem, Gribov horizon, first Gribov region
- Gribov-Zwanziger action: modified gluon propagator with complex poles
- Gribov propagator D(0)=0 PROVED (infrared suppression)
- Chiral anomaly: Adler-Bardeen exact, index theorem, Banks-Casher
- Witten-Veneziano: eta' mass from topology, positivity PROVED
- Dual superconductor: 't Hooft mechanism, abelian projection
- Center vortex: sine formula, trivial rep tension = 0 PROVED
- Confinement mechanisms landscape and mass gap problem summary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update Yang-Mills knowledge base (Parts XLIII-XLV)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add lattice Monte Carlo, large-N, stochastic quantization to Yang-Mills (Parts XLVI-XLIX)

Part XLVI: Lattice Monte Carlo - Wilson action, Metropolis algorithm,
Creutz ratio, strong coupling expansion, glueball mass extraction,
SU(3) glueball spectrum benchmarks.

Part XLVII: Large-N expansion - t Hooft coupling, genus expansion,
planar/non-planar classification, Eguchi-Kawai reduction, meson
amplitude suppression, baryon mass scaling.

Part XLVIII: Stochastic quantization - Langevin dynamics,
Fokker-Planck spectral gap, gauge-covariant Langevin (Zwanziger),
connection to regularity structures (Hairer), four characterizations
of mass gap.

Part XLIX: Complexity barriers - spectral gap undecidability
(Cubitt-Perez-Garcia-Wolf), QMA-hardness, derandomization,
pure YM no sign problem.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update Yang-Mills knowledge base (Parts XLVI-XLIX)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add SUSY Yang-Mills, AdS/CFT, constructive QFT to Yang-Mills (Parts L-LII)

Part L: Supersymmetric Yang-Mills - gluino condensate, Witten index,
Seiberg-Witten curve with monopole/dyon singularities, BPS mass formula,
soft breaking confinement, N=4 conformal no mass gap.

Part LI: AdS/CFT and holographic mass gap - AdS space, mass-dimension
relation, BF bound, hard-wall and soft-wall models, Witten model,
holographic entanglement, Hawking-Page deconfinement transition.

Part LII: Constructive QFT - rigorous 2D YM measure, spherical partition
function, Driver holonomy process, Levy Markov construction, heat kernel
on G, exact Wilson loop (proved bounded), CCHS regularity structures
construction, OS reconstruction with rigorous mass gap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update Yang-Mills knowledge base (Parts L-LII)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add functional RG, Kogut-Susskind, topology, grand summary to Yang-Mills (Parts LIII-LVI)

Part LIII: Functional RG - Wetterinck equation, dynamical gluon mass
generation, decoupling vs scaling solutions, Gaussian fixed point.

Part LIV: Kogut-Susskind Hamiltonian lattice - electric/magnetic terms,
strong coupling vacuum and mass gap, Gauss law constraint, no phase
transition conjecture.

Part LV: Topological aspects - instanton moduli space dimensions,
BPST instanton, topological susceptibility, Donaldson/Seiberg-Witten
invariants, theta dependence with min/max energy proved.

Part LVI: Grand summary - eight mass gap characterizations,
dimensional survey (2D solved, 3D partial, 4D open), requirements
for a complete 4D proof.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update Yang-Mills knowledge base (Parts LIII-LVI, comprehensive survey)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add stochastic NS, compressible NS, critical Sobolev to Navier-Stokes (Parts XXXI-XXXIII)

Part XXXI: Stochastic NS - martingale solutions, Flandoli-Romito
Markov selection, regularization by noise, ergodicity and mixing,
stochastic non-uniqueness, Malliavin calculus.

Part XXXII: Compressible NS - isentropic system, Lions-Feireisl weak
solutions (gamma > 3/2), incompressible limit (Ma -> 0), compressible
Euler blowup (MRRS 2022), NS-Fourier system.

Part XXXIII: Critical Sobolev framework - Sobolev embedding (p*=6 at
p=2), Gagliardo-Nirenberg-Sobolev, Ladyzhenskaya inequality, energy
inequality with energy_decreasing proved, Serrin gap = 1/2 proved,
critical spaces, Prodi-Serrin conditions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update Navier-Stokes knowledge base (Parts XXXI-XXXIII)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add modularity, Langlands, higher rank BSD (Parts XXXVIII-XL)

Part XXXVIII: Modularity theorem - weight-2 newforms, functional
equation, root number parity, modular parametrization, Ribet level
lowering and connection to FLT.

Part XXXIX: Langlands program - GL(2) correspondence, Galois
representations, Serre modularity (Khare-Wintenberger), generalized
BSD as Bloch-Kato, symmetric power L-functions (Sym^1 through Sym^4).

Part XL: Higher rank BSD - rank barrier (Euler systems limited to
rank 1), Nekovar height pairings, diagonal cycles (Gross-Kudla-Schoen),
Darmon-Rotger formula, higher Iwasawa theory, rank records.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update BSD knowledge base (Parts XXXVIII-XL)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add Yang-Mills Aristotle companion file (35 provable lemmas)

Companion file for automated proof search via Aristotle. Contains
routine supporting lemmas from the main YangMillsMassGap.lean:
- SU(N) Casimir eigenvalue computations (N=2,3,4)
- Beta function coefficients and positivity
- String tension calculations (2D Migdal formula)
- Heat kernel partition function values
- Lattice gauge theory numerics (plaquettes, Gauss law)
- Instanton moduli space dimensions
- Large-N genus expansion Euler characteristics
- Gribov-Zwanziger horizon condition values
- BF bound computation
- Real analysis supporting lemmas (exp, sin, cos, log)

All 35 lemmas use `sorry` - designed for Aristotle proof search.
No axioms, no definition sorries, no placeholders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update YM knowledge with Aristotle companion file

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add Aristotle companion files for BSD and Navier-Stokes

BirchSwinnertonDyerAristotle.lean (30+ lemmas):
- Hasse bound checks for 11a1 at small primes
- Discriminant and conductor computations
- Tunnell theorem arithmetic
- BSD leading coefficient arithmetic
- Mordell-Weil rank bounds
- Modular form level computations
- Galois representation determinant orders
- Symmetric power L-function degrees
- Iwasawa theory parameters

NavierStokesAristotle.lean (25+ lemmas):
- Sobolev embedding exponents (p*=6 at p=2, etc.)
- Serrin exponent pairs (p=6/q=3, p=4/q=8, energy gap)
- Kolmogorov scaling relations
- Onsager critical exponent and K41 connection
- She-Levesque intermittency model (zeta_3=1, zeta_6=16/9)
- Besov space critical smoothness
- Lions-Feireisl gamma thresholds
- Real analysis lemmas (Young, Cauchy-Schwarz, AM-GM, power mean)

All lemmas use sorry - designed for Aristotle proof search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add vorticity dynamics and bounded domains to Navier-Stokes (Parts XXXIV-XXXV)

Part XXXIV: Vorticity formulation - vortex stretching as key 3D
mechanism, 2D regularity from absence of stretching, enstrophy
production (proved stretching > dissipation implies growth), BKM
criterion, vortex filaments and reconnection, Constantin-Fefferman
direction criterion.

Part XXXV: Bounded domains - Prandtl boundary layer, Reynolds number
(proved positive), Kato inviscid limit criterion, Stokes operator
eigenvalues, exponential energy decay on bounded domains, bounded
domain summary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update NS knowledge base (Parts XXXIV-XXXV)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: add Goldfeld conjecture and computational BSD (Parts XLI-XLII)

Part XLI: Goldfeld conjecture - rank distribution (50/50 rank 0/1),
average rank 1/2, Bhargava-Shankar upper bounds via n-Selmer groups
(2-Selmer avg 3, 5-Selmer avg 6), positive rank 0 and rank 1
proportions, proved goldfeld_avg_rank = 1/2.

Part XLII: Computational BSD - verification data structure for
specific curves (11a1, 37a1, 389a1, 5077a1), computational
verification status for small conductors, parity conjecture
(Dokchitser-Dokchitser 2010), grand BSD summary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* research: update BSD knowledge base (Parts XLI-XLII, comprehensive)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Adding crossorigin="anonymous" to all tags that load XIVAPI icons fixes the broken images on GitHub Pages where coi-serviceworker injects COEP headers. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

Redesign chat flow to be step-by-step conversational

- Stage-based flow: initial → injury analyzed → video → reminder → done
- After image: brief injury summary + one exercise + ask for video
- After video: form score + corrections + ask about reminders
- After yes: collect email and send reminder
- Fix Resend from address to hello@optimalemails.xyz
- Placeholder text adapts to current stage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix workout logging buttons not clickable

Root causes fixed:
1. Removed HTML disabled attribute — buttons were permanently unresponsive to
   all pointer events when fields were empty; now always clickable
2. Validation moved into click handler — shows inline red error banner when
   weight/reps fields are empty instead of silently blocking the click
3. Supabase error feedback — handleCompleteSet/handleSaveAll now return true/false;
   cards show explicit error message if insert fails (e.g. workout_logs table missing)
4. Input type changed text→text with inputMode retained — type=number has known
   onChange quirks on mobile; type=text with inputMode gives numeric keyboard
   without the number-parsing edge cases
5. Input font-size raised to 1rem (16px) — prevents iOS Safari auto-zoom on
   focus which disrupts layout and makes other elements temporarily unreachable
6. Removed overflow:hidden from exercise cards — was not clipping but could
   mask future rendering issues; changed to overflow:visible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat!: add CREATED state, per-event Discord roles, and phase-based state naming

Refactor the event state machine to handle all Discord setup through
state transitions instead of synchronously in createEvent(). Events now
start in CREATED state and progress through INIT_CHANNEL → INIT_ROLES →
PLANNED via the poller, with retry on failure.

Each event gets three Discord roles (Accepted/Declined/Maybe) created
during INIT_ROLES and assigned on button clicks. Roles are cleaned up
during DELETE.

Renames states to follow phase-based convention: NOTIFIED → PRE_NOTIFIED,
ALBUM_READY → POST_ALBUM_READY, ALBUM_POSTED → POST_ALBUM_SHARED,
COMPLETED → POST_COMPLETED.

BREAKING CHANGE: EventState enum values renamed; DB migration handles
existing data.

commit.message:
Fix paper close deadlock — use RLock instead of Lock

_handle_exit() holds self._lock while calling patched _close_position_locked(),
which calls _broadcast() → get_state() → tries to re-acquire self._lock.
threading.Lock is not reentrant — same thread deadlocks on second acquire.
RLock allows the same thread to re-acquire, fixing close signal + auto-close on tick.

(props_invalid_value). Widened to string|number|null.

commit.message:
Fix Unicode sanitization to strip actual surrogate characters, not just escape sequences

The previous regex only matched literal \uDxxx text in strings. When
fetched web content contains actual surrogate code points (U+D800-DFFF),
JSON.stringify encodes them as \uDxxx escapes that PostgreSQL JSONB
rejects. Now also strips null characters (U+0000).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
